questions.question: LLMs are trained primarily using which objective?
options: Self-supervised next-token prediction on massive text
questions.explanation: Pre-training: predict the next token across billions of examples. No manual labels needed -- text itself provides supervision.

questions.question: Tokens in LLMs are typically:
questions.explanation: Byte-Pair Encoding creates subwords -- frequent words get 1 token, rare words split into multiple. Balances vocabulary size and coverage.

questions.question: Higher temperature in LLM sampling produces:
questions.explanation: Temperature scales logits before softmax. High temp = flatter distribution = more random sampling. Low temp = peaked = top token dominates.

questions.question: RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback) is used to:
options: Align model outputs with human preferences after pre-training
questions.explanation: RLHF: SFT -> reward model -> PPO. Makes models helpful, harmless, honest -- transforms raw pre-trained models into assistants.

questions.question: Open-source LLMs like LLaMA differ from GPT-4 in that they:
options: Can be downloaded, run locally, and fine-tuned on your hardware
questions.explanation: Open weights = download and run on your GPU. Important for privacy, cost, customisation. LLaMA-3 70B rivals GPT-4 on many benchmarks.

questions.question: Context window in an LLM refers to:
options: The maximum number of tokens the model processes in one call
questions.explanation: The context window limits how much text the model can 'see' at once. GPT-4: 128K tokens. Claude 3.5: 200K. Longer = more expensive.

questions.question: Hallucination in LLMs means:
options: The model is slow to respond
The model generates confident but factually incorrect information
questions.explanation: LLMs predict plausible-sounding tokens, not verified facts. Without retrieval/grounding, they can confidently invent citations, facts, code.

questions.question: The main advantage of smaller quantised models (GGUF, AWQ) is:
options: Run on consumer hardware (CPU/small GPU) with modest accuracy loss
questions.explanation: 4-bit quantisation reduces 7B model from 14GB to ~4GB. Runs on a laptop CPU. Opens LLMs to edge deployment.

questions.question: System prompts in chat models are used to:
options: Set the model's persona, constraints, and behaviour for all turns in a conversation
questions.explanation: The system prompt is injected before user messages. It sets role, tone, restrictions, and output format for the whole session.

questions.question: Which is NOT a current limitation of LLMs?
options: Cannot process any text at all
questions.explanation: LLMs process text very well -- that is their core capability. Limitations are: cutoff, hallucination, cost, context length, reasoning.
